F. O Flow-NTC open circuit NTC broken, NTC cable broken, Defective connection at NTC, Defective connection at electronics
F. 1 Return-NTC open circuit NTC broken, NTC cable broken, Defective connection at NTC, Defective connection at electronics
F.10 Flow NTC short circuit NTC defective, short circuit in cable harness, cable/casing short cut
F.11 Return NTC short circuit NTC defective, short circuit in cable harness, cable/casing short cut
F.13 Tank NTC short circuit NTC defective, short circuit in cable harness, cable/casing short cut

fault_codes.description: Safety temperature limiter by NTC activated
possible_causes: Flow-NTC not correctly thermal-connected or defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.20 Safety temperature limiter by NTC activated Flow-NTC not correctly thermal-connected or defective; appliance does not shut down

possible_causes: Too little water in the appliance
cable to pump or water sensor defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.22 Dry fire Too little water in the appliance, water pressure sensor defective, cable to pump or water sensor defective, pump blocked or defective, pump output too low

fault_codes.description: Water shortage, temperature difference between flow and return NTC too large

source_refs.source_quote: F.23 Water shortage, temperature difference between flow and return NTC too large Pump blocked or defective, pump output too low, flow and return NTC interchanged

fault_codes.description: Water shortage, temperature rise too quick

source_refs.source_quote: F.24 Water shortage, temperature rise too quick Pump blocked, insufficient pump output, air in appliance, water pressure too low
F.27 Incorrect sensing of flame Flame detector defective

fault_codes.description: Appliance does not start: Attempts to ignite during start failed
possible_causes: Faults in the gas supply such as:- Gas meter or gas pressure detector defective
igniter (ignition transformer, ignition cable, ignition plug) defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.28 Appliance does not start: Attempts to ignite during start failed Faults in the gas supply such as:- Gas meter or gas pressure detector defective - Air in gas- Gas flow pressure too low - Fire protection tap has disengaged Faults in the gas valve, wrong gas setting, igniter (ignition transformer, ignition cable, ignition plug) defective, ionisation current stopped (cable, electrode), faulty earthing in appliance, electronics defective

fault_codes.description: Flame goes off during operation and subsequent ignition attempts failed

source_refs.source_quote: F.29 Flame goes off during operation and subsequent ignition attempts failed Gas supply temporarily stopped, faulty earthing of appliance

possible_causes: plug not inserted correctly on fan

source_refs.source_quote: F.32 Fan speed variation Fan blocked, plug not inserted correctly on fan, hall sensor defective, fault in cable harness, electronics defective

possible_causes: two power sources on eBUS with different polarity

source_refs.source_quote: F.49 eBUS under voltage Short circuit on eBUS, overload on eBUS or two power sources on eBUS with different polarity

possible_causes: Short circuit/earth (ground) leak in cable harness to gas valves
gas valve assembly defective (earth/ground leak from coils)

source_refs.source_quote: F.61 Gas-valve control defective Short circuit/earth (ground) leak in cable harness to gas valves, gas valve assembly defective (earth/ground leak from coils), electronic control system defective
F.62 Gas valve shut off delay Gas valve leaking, electronics defective
F.63 EEPROM error Electronics defective

possible_causes: Short-circuit in flow or return NTC or electronics defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.64 Electronics/NTC fault Short-circuit in flow or return NTC or electronics defective

possible_causes: Electronics too hot due to external effect

source_refs.source_quote: F.65 Electronics temperature too high Electronics too hot due to external effect, electronics defective

fault_codes.description: Flame detector input signal is outside the limits (0 or 5 V)

source_refs.source_quote: F.67 Flame detector input signal is outside the limits (O or 5 V) Electronics defective

fault_codes.description: No valid DSN in display and/or main board
possible_causes: Spare part failure display and main board interchanged at same time and device specific number not adjusted

source_refs.source_quote: F.70 No valid DSN in display and/or main board Spare part failure display and main board interchanged at same time and device specific number not adjusted

fault_codes.description: Flow NTC reports constant value (stuck at)

source_refs.source_quote: F.71 Flow NTC reports constant value (stuck at) Flow NTC is defective

possible_causes: Flow and/or return NTC is defective (tolerances too big)

source_refs.source_quote: F.72 Flow and/or return NTC fault Flow and/or return NTC is defective (tolerances too big)

fault_codes.description: No pressure rise was detected on turning on the pump
possible_causes: Water pressure sensor or/and pump defective
connect external expansion vessel to return

source_refs.source_quote: F.75 No pressure rise was detected on turning on the pump Water pressure sensor or/and pump defective Air in the heating system, quick bleeder defective Too little water in appliance; check adjustable by-pass; connect external expansion vessel to return

fault_codes.description: Overheating protection on primary heat exchanger triggered
possible_causes: Cable or cable connection of fuse in the primary heat exchanger defective

source_refs.source_quote: F.76 Overheating protection on primary heat exchanger triggered Cable or cable connection of fuse in the primary heat exchanger defective, or primary heat exchanger defective

fault_codes.description: Condensate pump or feedback of accessories blocks heating
possible_causes: Condensate pump defective or flume flap feedback triggered

source_refs.source_quote: F.77 Condensate pump or feedback of accessories blocks heating Condensate pump defective or flume flap feedback triggered

possible_causes: link box VR65 connected to combination boiler

source_refs.source_quote: F.78 Wrong configuration with accessory link box VR65 connected to combination boiler
con No communication to main board Connection error display main board

diagnostic_codes.description: Operating modes of the two-speed heating pump
diagnostic_codes.value_range: O = pre ignition speed 1, hot water or heating speed 2, overrun speed 1; 1 = pre ignition speed 1, hot water speed 2, heating speed 1 overrun speed 1; 2 = like 1, but speed in heating mode dependent on heating part load d. O (if d.0 is below 60% of full load, then pump speed 1, other-wise speed 2); 3 = always speed 2

source_refs.table_title: Table 10.3 Diagnostics codes of the second diagnostic level
source_refs.source_quote: d.19 Operating modes of the two-speed heating pump O = pre ignition speed 1, hot water or heating speed 2, overrun speed 1 1 = pre ignition speed 1, hot water speed 2, heating speed 1 overrun speed 1 2 = like 1, but speed in heating mode dependent on heating part load d. O (if d.0 is below 60% of full load, then pump speed 1, other-wise speed 2) 3 = always speed 2 (factory setting: 2)

safety_warnings.text: Risk of burns or scalding!

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of burns or scalding!

safety_warnings.text: Potentially dangerous situations for the product and environment.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Potentially dangerous situations for the product and environment.

safety_warnings.text: Useful information and instructions.

source_refs.source_quote: Note! Useful information and instructions.

safety_warnings.text: This boiler must be installed and serviced by a competent person in accordance with the Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulations 1998. In the UK 'CORGI' registered installers undertake the work in compliance with safe and satisfactory standard.

source_refs.source_quote: Note! This boiler must be installed and serviced by a competent person in accordance with the Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulations 1998. In the UK 'CORGI' registered installers undertake the work in compliance with safe and satisfactory standard.

safety_warnings.text: Any incorrect use is forbidden.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Any incorrect use is forbidden.

safety_warnings.text: If you smell gas or suspect a gas leak: • Do not switch lights on or off. • Do not use any other electrical switches. • Do not use a telephone in the hazardous area. • Do not use naked flames, such as matches or cigarette lighters. • Do not smoke. • Turn off the gas supply at the gas meter. • Open the windows and doors. • Warn other residents. • Get out of the house. • Consult your gas supplier, service agent or other competent person.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Smell of gas. Risk of poisoning and explosion due to a malfunction If you smell gas or suspect a gas leak: • Do not switch lights on or off. • Do not use any other electrical switches. • Do not use a telephone in the hazardous area. • Do not use naked flames, such as matches or cigarette lighters. • Do not smoke. • Turn off the gas supply at the gas meter. • Open the windows and doors. • Warn other residents. • Get out of the house. • Consult your gas supplier, service agent or other competent person.

safety_warnings.text: This boiler must be earthed.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger This boiler must be earthed.

safety_warnings.text: When tightening or slackening screwed connections always use suitable open-ended spanners (not pipe wrench, or extensions, etc.). Incorrect use and/or unsuitable tools can lead to damage being caused (e.g. gas or water leakage)!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! When tightening or slackening screwed connections always use suitable open-ended spanners (not pipe wrench, or extensions, etc.). Incorrect use and/or unsuitable tools can lead to damage being caused (e.g. gas or water leakage)!

safety_warnings.text: Do not !install the appliance in rooms prone to frost. In rooms with aggressive steam or dust, the appliance must be operated independent of the ambient air.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution Do not !install the appliance in rooms prone to frost. In rooms with aggressive steam or dust, the appliance must be operated independent of the ambient air.

safety_warnings.text: The appliance must be installed and serviced by a competent person as stated in the Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulations 1998. In IE, the installation must be in accordance with the current edition of I.S.813 'Domestic Gas Installations', the current Building Regulations and reference should be made to the current ETCI rules for electrical installation.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! The appliance must be installed and serviced by a competent person as stated in the Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulations 1998. In IE, the installation must be in accordance with the current edition of I.S.813 'Domestic Gas Installations', the current Building Regulations and reference should be made to the current ETCI rules for electrical installation.

safety_warnings.text: Vaillant appliances are certified only for use with genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Only use genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Malfunctions can occur if you use other accessories. These may result in damage and injury. You will find a list of genuine flue pipes in the Vaillant installation manual for flue pipes. The CE mark is valid only if the appliance is operated with Vaillant flue pipes.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Vaillant appliances are certified only for use with genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Only use genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Malfunctions can occur if you use other accessories. These may result in damage and injury. You will find a list of genuine flue pipes in the Vaillant installation manual for flue pipes. The CE mark is valid only if the appliance is operated with Vaillant flue pipes.

safety_warnings.text: Single feed indirect cylinders are not suitable. The domestic hot water cylinder must be of the double feed fully indirect coil type. It must be suitable for working at a gauge pressure of 0.35 bar above the safety valve setting.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Single feed indirect cylinders are not suitable. The domestic hot water cylinder must be of the double feed fully indirect coil type. It must be suitable for working at a gauge pressure of 0.35 bar above the safety valve setting.

safety_warnings.text: It is ESSENTIAL that the cleanser is fully removed from the system after flushing and before adding inhibitor. Take care to ensure that all low points in the system are fully drained.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! It is ESSENTIAL that the cleanser is fully removed from the system after flushing and before adding inhibitor. Take care to ensure that all low points in the system are fully drained.

safety_warnings.text: When installing the appliance, care should be taken to avoid any possibility of personal injury when handling sheet metal parts.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! When installing the appliance, care should be taken to avoid any possibility of personal injury when handling sheet metal parts.

safety_warnings.text: Vaillant appliances are certified only for use with genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Only use genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Malfunctions can occur if you use other accessories. These may result in damage and injury. You will find a list of genuine flue pipes in the Vaillant installation manual for flue pipes. The CE mark is valid only if the appliance is operated with Vaillant flue pipes.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Vaillant appliances are certified only for use with genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Only use genuine Vaillant flue pipes. Malfunctions can occur if you use other accessories. These may result in damage and injury. You will find a list of genuine flue pipes in the Vaillant installation manual for flue pipes. The CE mark is valid only if the appliance is operated with Vaillant flue pipes.

safety_warnings.text: The gas connection may only be made by a competent person. The legal directives and the local regulations for gas supply companies must be observed.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! The gas connection may only be made by a competent person. The legal directives and the local regulations for gas supply companies must be observed.

safety_warnings.text: Ensure a stress-relief assembly of the gas pipes to avoid leakages!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Ensure a stress-relief assembly of the gas pipes to avoid leakages!

safety_warnings.text: The gas regulating block may be tested for leakage only with a maximum pressure of 150 mbar! Higher testing pressures can damage the gas fitting.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! The gas regulating block may be tested for leakage only with a maximum pressure of 150 mbar! Higher testing pressures can damage the gas fitting.

safety_warnings.text: When making final connection to the boiler, if using soldered fittings, extra care should be taken to avoid damage to isolation valves through heat transfer. Before connection check the supply of local gas.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! When making final connection to the boiler, if using soldered fittings, extra care should be taken to avoid damage to isolation valves through heat transfer. Before connection check the supply of local gas.

safety_warnings.text: When making final connection to the boiler, if using soldered fittings, extra care should be taken to avoid damage through heat transfer.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! When making final connection to the boiler, if using soldered fittings, extra care should be taken to avoid damage through heat transfer.

safety_warnings.text: This appliance must be earthed. Electrocution caused by touching live parts can be fatal. Before working on the appliance, turn off the power supply and secure against restart.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! This appliance must be earthed. Electrocution caused by touching live parts can be fatal. Before working on the appliance, turn off the power supply and secure against restart.

safety_warnings.text: This appliance must be wired in accordance with these instructions. Any fault arising from incorrect wiring cannot be put right under the terms of the Vaillant guarantee.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! This appliance must be wired in accordance with these instructions. Any fault arising from incorrect wiring cannot be put right under the terms of the Vaillant guarantee.

safety_warnings.text: Mains connection terminals L and N remain live "unless isolated at the fused spur or electrical outlet supplying the boiler".

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Mains connection terminals L and N remain live "unless isolated at the fused spur or electrical outlet supplying the boiler".

safety_warnings.text: Do not connect any mains 230V power to the connections 7-8-9 or BUS (+,-).

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Do not connect any mains 230V power to the connections 7-8-9 or BUS (+,-).

safety_warnings.text: Do not connect supply voltage! Risk of damage to electronics!

source_refs.source_quote: Caution: Do not connect supply voltage! Risk of damage to electronics!

safety_warnings.text: DO NOT proceed with the adjustment or attempt to put the unit into service if the inlet working pressure lies outside the 17-25 mbar range.

source_refs.source_quote: Natural gas: DO NOT proceed with the adjustment or attempt to put the unit into service if the inlet working pressure lies outside the 17-25 mbar range.

safety_warnings.text: DO NOT proceed with the adjustment or attempt to put the unit into service if the inlet working pressure is lower than 34 mbar.

source_refs.source_quote: LPG: DO NOT proceed with the adjustment or attempt to put the unit into service if the inlet working pressure is lower than 34 mbar.

safety_warnings.text: Before operating the boiler check the type plate and ensure that the correct gas type

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Before operating the boiler check the type plate and ensure that the correct gas type

safety_warnings.text: Inappropriate modifications can cause damage. If your boiler still does not operate then please consult the trouble shooting section of this literature.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! Inappropriate modifications can cause damage. If your boiler still does not operate then please consult the trouble shooting section of this literature.

safety_warnings.text: If the boiler still shuts off after three attempts of resetting, please consult the trouble shooting section of this literature.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! If the boiler still shuts off after three attempts of resetting, please consult the trouble shooting section of this literature.

safety_warnings.text: After converting from natural gas to LPG, commission and check boiler function as described in commissioning section of the servicing and installation instructions.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! After converting from natural gas to LPG, commission and check boiler function as described in commissioning section of the servicing and installation instructions.

safety_warnings.text: This conversion must only be carried out by a competent person in accordance with the Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulation 1998. In the UK CORGI registered installers undertake the work to a safe and satisfactory standard.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! This conversion must only be carried out by a competent person in accordance with the Gas Safety (Installation and Use) Regulation 1998. In the UK CORGI registered installers undertake the work to a safe and satisfactory standard.

safety_warnings.text: It is law that all servicing work is carried out by a competent person (CORGI registered). Inspections/Maintenance work not carried out by a competent person can result in damage to property and personal injury.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! It is law that all servicing work is carried out by a competent person (CORGI registered). Inspections/Maintenance work not carried out by a competent person can result in damage to property and personal injury.

safety_warnings.text: Electrocution by touching live parts can be fatal! The supply terminals in the appliance are permanently live unless the appliance is isolated from the electrical supply either by removing the fuse from a fused spur or pulling the plug out if connected to a socket outlet. Protect the electronics box from spray water. Before working on the appliance, turn off the power supply and secure against restart!

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Electrocution by touching live parts can be fatal! The supply terminals in the appliance are permanently live unless the appliance is isolated from the electrical supply either by removing the fuse from a fused spur or pulling the plug out if connected to a socket outlet. Protect the electronics box from spray water. Before working on the appliance, turn off the power supply and secure against restart!

safety_warnings.topic: Burner door seal and nuts replacement
safety_warnings.text: The burner door seal and combustion chamber burner door securing nuts on the burner module, see figs 8.6 and 8.7 must be replaced each time the module is removed for example during maintenance if the burner flange insulation shows any signs of damage or small cracks it must also be replaced.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution! The burner door seal and combustion chamber burner door securing nuts on the burner module, see figs 8.6 and 8.7 must be replaced each time the module is removed for example during maintenance if the burner flange insulation shows any signs of damage or small cracks it must also be replaced.

maintenance_tasks.description: Ensure it is not blocked, damaged and is fitted correctly.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Check the air/ gas flue system and ensure it is not blocked, damaged and is fitted correctly.

maintenance_tasks.description: Measure the gas rate during operation (see table 5.1. inside section 5 commissioning part I). If the gas rate is lower than the minimum gas rate, follow the maintenance schedule in column 2.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Measure the gas rate during operation (see table 5.1. inside section 5 commissioning part I). If the gas rate is lower than the minimum gas rate, follow the maintenance schedule in column 2.

maintenance_tasks.description: Check combustion by measuring CO and CO2 values, compare to table 9.1. If the measurement is outside the tolerances follow the maintenance schedule in column 2. If a flue gas analyser is not available check the ignition and burner flame picture through the viewing window if incomplete combustion is evident perform the maintenance schedule in column 2.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Check combustion by measuring CO and CO2 values, compare to table 9.1. If the measurement is outside the tolerances follow the maintenance schedule in column 2. If a flue gas analyser is not available check the ignition and burner flame picture through the viewing window if incomplete combustion is evident perform the maintenance schedule in column 2.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Isolate appliance from electrical mains and gas supply

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Isolate the appliance from the electrical mains supply, close the gas service valve.

maintenance_tasks.description: Visually inspect the general heat exchanger area for signs of corrosion, sooting or other forms of damage. If damage is evident perform the tasks in the maintenance schedule in column 2.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Visually inspect the general heat exchanger area for signs of corrosion, sooting or other forms of damage. If damage is evident perform the tasks in the maintenance schedule in column 2.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Remove and clean burner module and primary heat exchanger
maintenance_tasks.description: Remove the burner module (as described in section 8.1.6.) if maintenance schedule is required from steps 2, 3 or 5 above. Clean the primary heat exchanger. Fit new burner door seal kit (observe the assembly instructions enclosed with the kit). Refit the burner module and tighten the nuts.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Remove the burner module (as described in section 8.1.6.) if maintenance schedule is required from steps 2, 3 or 5 above. Clean the primary heat exchanger. Fit new burner door seal kit (observe the assembly instructions enclosed with the kit). Refit the burner module and tighten the nuts.

maintenance_tasks.description: Check all appliance electrical connections and make adjustments, if necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Check all appliance electrical connections and make adjustments, if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.description: If applicable check/re-pressurise system expansion vessel as necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: If applicable check/re-pressurise system expansion vessel as necessary.

maintenance_tasks.description: Check the appliance generally, check for dirt/dust and clean if necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Check the appliance generally, check for dirt/dust and clean if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Check and clean condensate trap and flexible condensate hose

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Check and clean condensate trap and flexible condensate hose.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Perform test operation and bleed system
maintenance_tasks.description: Perform a test operation of the appliance including the heating and hot water systems and bleed thew system if necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Perform a test operation of the appliance including the heating and hot water systems and bleed thew system if necessary.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Measure gas rate during operation (tolerance check)
maintenance_tasks.description: Measure the gas rate during operation and ensure it is inside the tolerances specified (see table 5.1 inside section 5 commissioning part I).

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Measure the gas rate during operation and ensure it is inside the tolerances specified (see table 5.1 inside section 5 commissioning part I).

maintenance_tasks.description: Re-check combustion by measuring CO and CO2 values, compare to table 9.1. Ensure the measurement is within the tolerances. If a flue gas analyser is not available check the ignition and burner flame picture visually. through the viewing window.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Re-check combustion by measuring CO and CO2 values, compare to table 9.1. Ensure the measurement is within the tolerances. If a flue gas analyser is not available check the ignition and burner flame picture visually. through the viewing window.

maintenance_tasks.description: Check boiler for leaks of any kind, rectify as necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Check boiler for leaks of any kind, rectify as necessary.

source_refs.section_title: Overview of the inspection and maintenance tasks
source_refs.source_quote: Complete gas boiler commissioning checklist (Benchmark).

maintenance_tasks.description: Release the pressure from the boiler. Remove valve cap from expansion vessel charge point. Check that the internal charge pressure of the expansion vessel is to the correct design pressure. If the pressure is lower than this the vessel should be re pressurised using an air pump. Refit the valve cap. Re pressurise boiler and heating system.

source_refs.source_quote: It is not necessary to perform this check every year a check every three years is sufficient. • Release the pressure from the boiler. • Remove valve cap from expansion vessel charge point. • Check that the internal charge pressure of the expansion vessel is to the correct design pressure. If the pressure is lower than this the vessel should be re pressurised using an air pump. • Refit the valve cap. • Re pressurise boiler and heating system.
